description: Cross-language test vectors for the Proof-of-Context wire format. Each vector specifies a payload and the expected canonical JSON serialization (sorted keys, no whitespace, RFC 8259) and the expected SHA-256 hash of the canonical JSON. Reference implementations across JavaScript, TypeScript, and Rust must all produce these exact values for the convergence claim of paper #2 (Agent Economy Infrastructure) §7.2 to hold empirically. Vectors are authoritative; if an implementation disagrees, the implementation has drifted from the spec.

vectors.description: Two-field flat object with mixed key alphabetic order. The canonical form sorts to price_usd then token. Tests basic key-sorting.
Object with a nested object and an array. The canonical form must sort the outer keys, then sort the nested object's keys, while preserving array order. Tests recursive sorting.
Empty object. Must canonicalize to the two-character JSON literal {}. Tests the boundary case.

Proof-of-context f_i input-manifest root, single source. The manifest commits to the set of input-world sources an inference consumed; each descriptor is {endpoint, payload_hash, source_id}, and the manifest is {sources:[...], version:"f_i/0.1"}. CONTRACT: the sources array MUST be pre-sorted ascending by (endpoint, payload_hash) BEFORE canonicalization. canonical-JSON sorts object keys but NOT array elements, so this array ordering is an additional cross-language contract point beyond v1-v3.

Proof-of-context f_i input-manifest root, two sources. Exercises the array pre-sort: sources are shown here in their REQUIRED post-pre-sort order (ascending by (endpoint, payload_hash); "/api/v1/gas" precedes "/api/v1/prices"). An implementation that builds the manifest from sources in any other order must pre-sort to this order before canonicalization to reproduce the hash.

Proof-of-context f_m canonical model lineage. Each epoch is {weights_hash, epoch, activation_block}; the lineage is {epochs:[...], version:"f_m/0.1"}. CONTRACT: epochs MUST be pre-sorted ascending by epoch before canonicalization. The committee/quorum publishers sign the canonical JSON of this snapshot; model_epoch_distance is computed from lineage positions and activation blocks.

notes: These vectors test the canonical JSON + SHA-256 stage of the wire format ONLY. Signature production is per-key and is tested in each implementation's own suite against a fixed test key.

ARRAY PRE-SORT CONTRACT (v4-v6): canonical-JSON sorts object KEYS but preserves ARRAY order. The f_i manifest `sources` must be pre-sorted ascending by (endpoint, payload_hash), and the f_m lineage `epochs` ascending by epoch, BEFORE canonicalization. The payloads above are shown already in that order; an implementation building from unsorted input must pre-sort to match the hash.

Implementations that pass these three vectors are confirmed to share the canonical-hash construction. Implementations that fail any vector have drifted and the cross-implementation verification chain may be broken.
When v0.2 of the spec lands, vectors are added; existing vectors are not removed. The vector file is append-only across spec revisions.
Authoritative source: this file in the proof-of-context repository on the main branch.
